title: Helmet with mechanism for cooling
abstract: A helmet with a mechanism for cooling comprises an inlet for allowing external air to flow into air pathways in the helmet, and a pad to hold moisture (liquid) to cool the air after the air has entered the helmet via the inlet. The helmet also contains a reservoir to hold the liquid, and a channel from the reservoir to the pad to transfer the liquid for providing moisture to the pad. Flowing air, cooled by the moistened pad, provides cooling to the wearer of the helmet. In an embodiment, a headgear includes a helmet and a cooling unit. The cooling unit is designed to be attachable to, and detachable from, the helmet by the user during normal use of the headgear.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A helmet comprising: an inlet for allowing external air to flow into air pathways in the helmet; and a pad to hold moisture to cool the air after the air has entered the helmet via the inlet. 2. The helmet of claim 1, wherein the helmet comprises a shell and an external attachment, wherein the inlet is an opening in the external attachment. 3. The helmet of claim 2, further comprising: a reservoir to hold a liquid; and a channel from the reservoir to the pad to transfer the liquid for providing moisture to the pad. 4. The helmet of claim 3, wherein each of the reservoir and the channel is contained in the external attachment. 5. The helmet of claim 3, wherein the reservoir is separate from the external attachment, the reservoir being attachable to the shell. 6. The helmet of claim 5, wherein the liquid is transferred to the pad via the channel based on capillary action. 7. The helmet of claim 5, further comprising a pump to push the liquid to the pad via the channel. 8. The helmet of claim 7, further comprising a compartment disposed between the reservoir and the channel, the compartment consisting of a locking mechanism operable to block passage of the liquid from the reservoir to the channel. 9. The helmet of claim 8, further comprising a suction mechanism to pull air external to the helmet towards the pad. 10. The helmet of claim 9, wherein the suction mechanism comprises 11. The helmet of claim 6, wherein the channel is a wick operative to wet the cooler pad through capillary action. 12. The helmet of claim 2, wherein the air pathways comprise grooves in an inner shell comprised in the shell. 13. The helmet of claim 1, wherein the inlet is a vent on an outer shell of the helmet, wherein the pad is disposed between the vent and the head of a person wearing the helmet. 14. The helmet of claim 13, further comprising one or more stoppers attached to an inner surface of the inner shell to provide a further part of the air pathway in continuation of the vent. 15. The helmet of claim 14, further comprising a suction mechanism to pull air towards the pad via the air pathway. 16. The helmet of claim 15, further comprising: a reservoir to hold liquid; and a channel from the reservoir to the pad to transfer the liquid for providing moisture to the pad. 17. The helmet of claim 16, wherein the liquid is transferred via the channel based on capillary action. 18. The helmet of claim 17, further comprising a pump to push the liquid to the pad via the channel. 19. The helmet of claim 18, further comprising one of an electronic control circuit and a timer circuit to regulate the transfer of liquid to the pad. 20. The helmet of claim 19, wherein the outer shell of the helmet is made of one of ABS (Acrylonitrile Butadiene Styrene) and carbon fiber, wherein the inner shell of the helmet is made of EPS (Expandable Poly Styrene). neactgear comprising: a helmet: and 

 a cooling unit, wherein the cooling unit is designed to be attachable to, and detachable from, said helmet by a user of said headgear during normal use of said headgear by said user. 22. The headgear of claim 21, wherein said cooling unit comprises: one or more inlets; a fan for drawing in air into said cooling unit via said one or more inlets; a pad to hold moisture to cool the air drawn into said cooling unit to generate cooled air; and an air outlet to direct said cooled air into said helmet. 23. The headgear of claim 22, wherein said fan and said pad are housed within a main housing unit of said cooling unit, wherein said air outlet is a separate component connected to said main housing unit. 24. The headgear of claim 23, wherein the cooling unit further comprises a reservoir to hold a cooling fluid, wherein said reservoir is connected to said pad through a channel to allow said cooling fluid to moisten said pad. 25. The headgear of claim 24, wherein said cooling unit is attachable to said helmet by an elastic band. 26. A cooling unit comprising: one or more inlets; a fan for drawing in air into said cooling unit via said one or more inlets; a pad to hold moisture to cool the air drawn into said cooling unit to generate cooled air; and an air outlet to direct said cooled air out of said cooling unit, wherein said cooling unit is designed to be attachable to, and detachable from, a helmet by a user of said helmet during normal use. 27. The cooling unit of claim 26, wherein said fan and said pad are housed within a main housing unit of said cooling unit, wherein said air outlet is a separate component connected to said main housing unit. 28. The cooling unit of claim 27, further comprising a reservoir to hold a cooling fluid, wherein said reservoir is connected to said pad through a channel to allow said cooling fluid to moisten said pad. 29. The cooling unit of claim 28, wherein the cooling unit is attachable to said helmet by an elastic band. 30. An object comprising: a user-wearable component; and a cooling unit, wherein the cooling unit is designed to be attachable to, and detachable from, said user- wearable component by a user of said object during normal use of said object by said user. 31. The object of claim 30, wherein said user- wearable component is one of an apparel and a cap. 32. The object of claim 31, wherein the cooling unit comprises: one or more inlets; a fan for drawing in air into said cooling unit via said one or more inlets; a pad to hold moisture to cool the air drawn into said cooling unit to generate cooled air; and an air outlet to direct said cooled air out of said cooling unit. 33. The object of claim 32, wherein said fan and said pad are housed within a main housing unit of said cooling unit, wherein said air outlet is a separate component connected to said main housing unit. 34. The object of claim 33, further comprising a reservoir to hold a cooling fluid, wherein said reservoir is connected to said pad through a channel to allow said cooling fluid to moisten said pad.
